title: Exclude Soft-Deleted Objects from ERD
story: As a DataTools Pro user viewing an ERD, I want soft-deleted objects to be excluded from the diagram, so that I only see active objects and their relationships.
description:
## Bug
When an object is deleted from the Dictionary, it still appears in the ERD because deletion uses soft-delete and the ERD query does not filter by deletion status.

## Root Cause
The ERD query fetches from `dtp_object_fields` using only `user_id` and data type filters, without joining to the `scorecards` table to check soft-delete status.

## Fix
Add a JOIN to the `scorecards` table and filter out records where the scorecard has been soft-deleted (`deleted_at IS NULL` or equivalent).
acceptance_criteria: Soft-deleted objects do not appear in the ERD
ERD query joins to the scorecards table to check deletion status
Active objects and their relationships continue to display correctly
Performance impact of the additional join is minimal
ux_changes: Soft-deleted objects no longer appear in the ERD diagram.
middleware_changes: ERD data query updated to JOIN the scorecards table and filter out soft-deleted records.
implementation_notes: The existing query on dtp_object_fields was updated to include a JOIN to the scorecards table, filtering where deleted_at IS NULL. Developer confirmed this was a soft-delete issue and updated the query accordingly.
comment_summary: Developer confirmed soft-delete was the cause and added a join to the scorecards table to filter deleted objects. Query was updated and deployed to staging for review.
